subject: Ecology
title: Swamps
description: A quick, practical guide to Swamps.

hook.text: A swamp is a wetland dominated by trees—dark, flooded, and dripping with life.

buildup.text: Cypress swamps in the American South are iconic—bald cypress trees with knobby 'knees' rising from water.

discovery.text: Swamp trees have adaptations for waterlogged roots: buttress trunks, pneumatophores, and aerenchyma.

twist.text: Tropical peat swamps in Borneo store more carbon per hectare than any other forest type.

climax.text: Swamps harbor species found nowhere else—including the ghost orchid of Florida's Everglades.

punchline.text: A swamp is a forest that learned to swim.

quiz.question: What are cypress knees?
options: Root structures that project above swamp water
